must_detect.matched_text:
Processor will treat any non-public information expected to have a material effect on the price or value of Karrinup BioDevices Ltd’s quoted products as market sensitive and “not generally available” within the meaning of the Corporations Act (Cth), including project codenames (e.g., Project Kestrel), unannounced earnings ranges, or terms of a pending placement.
No selective disclosure: any market sensitive information must not be disclosed to any third party unless and until it has been released on the market announcements platform in accordance with continuous disclosure obligations (Listing Rule 3.1 timing) or a cleansing notice has been lodged, and Processor personnel must not trade in our securities while in possession of such information (insider trading prohibitions ss1042A–1043A).
Blackout windows apply to all Processor staff with access to finance, HR or board materials: from 30 days before half-year and full-year results until the close of trading on the day after the announcement, and during any period notified by the Company Secretary; pre-briefings may only occur using scripts that mirror the lodged announcement.
Subtle note: payroll and recruitment files may embed MNPI (e.g., offer letters and start dates for a new CFO not yet announced), and aggregated device shipment reports may be price sensitive if they reveal performance trends not generally available.
Public-information carve-out: information contained in our Appendix 4E for FY25 lodged on 29 Aug 2026 and the investor presentation released on 17 May 2025 are generally available and may be disclosed; the media summary issued on 01 Mar 2024 is stale and should not be relied upon for current processing instructions.
For precision: Pindarra’s internal office relocation notice dated 12 Sep 2025 is a non-issuer operational update and is not relevant to MNPI controls under this DPA.
Cross-border disclosures: Processor will host primary systems in Australia; any overseas disclosure under APP 8 (e.g., support access from New Zealand) requires prior written consent, documented safeguards, and Processor assumes s16C accountability for breaches by overseas recipients.
Security and breach: Processor must maintain ISO-27001-aligned controls and notify Karrinup BioDevices Ltd within 48 hours of becoming aware of an eligible data breach, with content sufficient for OAIC notification if required.
Retention: unless otherwise directed, retain HR and WHS health records for 7 years after employment ends, credit assessment records for 7 years after account closure, and transactional billing logs for 7 years; securely destroy or de-identify data thereafter, with certificates of destruction on request.
Data subject rights: Processor will assist Karrinup BioDevices Ltd to respond to access and correction requests under APP 12 and APP 13 within 30 days; all direct requests received by Processor must be forwarded without delay and not answered except to acknowledge receipt.
Public filing reference for status checks: see the Director’s Appendix 3Y lodged on 14 Dec 2025 for already-announced changes in interests; draft board papers discussing prospective issuances remain not generally available until an announcement is released.
Points of contact: Ms. Tamsin Rorke, Privacy Officer, privacy@karrinupbiodevices.example.au; and Dr. Owen Talford, Compliance Lead at Pindarra Data Services Pty Ltd, complianc
